title: matthiola tristis var. coronopifolia
intro: Matthiola tristis var. coronopifolia, also known as the Stock or Garden Stock, is a perennial plant species in the Brassicaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The plant has been used to support respiratory health, particularly in relieving coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Matthiola tristis var. coronopifolia are typically used to create infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its health benefits.
